---
title: "get_raw_data"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# ---- Setup ----

library(tidyverse)
library(rgbif)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(janitor)
library(tigris)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE) #store downloaded shapefiles locally 

# Set a seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(123)

```

```{r}
# ---- Paths ----

dir_raw       <- "data/raw"
dir_gbif      <- file.path(dir_raw, "gbif")
dir_viirs     <- file.path(dir_raw, "viirs")
dir_boundaries<- file.path(dir_raw, "boundaries")

dir.create(dir_raw,       showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(dir_gbif,      showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(dir_viirs,     showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(dir_boundaries,showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# ---- 2. GBIF Bat Occurrence Data ----

#We will focus on these 3 bat species in CA:

bat_species <- c(
  "Myotis yumanensis",
  "Myotis californicus",
  "Lasiurus cinereus"
)


# GBIF (Global Biodiversity Information Facility) is an international database of biodiversity records.
# Each species in GBIF is assigned a unique numeric identifier: "speciesKey",
# which we must use when requesting occurrence data through the GBIF API.

bat_taxa <- map_df(bat_species, ~ {
  rgbif::name_backbone(name = .x) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    select(scientificName, speciesKey, rank, status)
})

bat_taxa
bat_keys <- bat_taxa$speciesKey
print(bat_keys)

```
